steps.title: Follow the series argument
steps.content: The passage says each Aubrey/Maturin novel ends abruptly, but the next book continues where the prior one leaves off. Therefore, the abrupt endings are a problem only if the books are treated separately.

steps.title: Choose the continuous-story claim
steps.content: Choice A invents an unfair reputation comparison. Choice B narrows the point to two complex novels. Choice D introduces reader enjoyment and flaws, which are not discussed. The passage argues that the series functions as one long work. The answer is C.
